text.title: Laravel Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for layered Laravel architecture.

rule.statement: Controllers MUST NOT call Eloquent (`Model::query()`, `Model::find()`, `Model::create()`) directly. All data access flows through a service method.
rule.rationale: Eloquent calls in controllers leak DB concerns into HTTP layer; transactions span poorly; tests require full HTTP stack.

rule.statement: Every controller action accepting a request body MUST type-hint a Form Request class (extending `FormRequest`) with `authorize()` + `rules()`.
rule.rationale: Form Requests separate validation + authorization from business logic; tested in isolation; messages localizable.

rule.statement: JSON responses MUST be serialized via API Resource (`JsonResource`) or Resource Collection. Direct `return $model;` or `return response()->json($model->toArray())` is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Resources decouple API shape from schema; control hidden/visible fields per consumer.

rule.statement: Every service method that performs multiple Eloquent writes MUST wrap them in `DB::transaction(fn() => ...)`. Controllers MUST NOT open transactions.
rule.rationale: Service is the unit-of-work boundary; controller-level transactions span HTTP details and break with redirects.

rule.statement: Resource-level authorization MUST live in a Policy class (`UserPolicy`). Form Request `authorize()` MUST call `$this->user()->can('update', $resource)`.
rule.rationale: Policies centralize permission logic; reusable across web + API + console; testable.
